page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is a general-purpose server powered by a custom AWS Graviton processor using the 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 1 dedicated physical core with 1 thread, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and includes 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6gd.medium demonstrates average performance in single-core CPU workloads, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1493.49. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core and overall throughput metrics fall into lower performance tiers. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 926.0 and a multi-core score of 923.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 59.37 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and drops to 19.34 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, with write speeds at 9.33 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities include 1.18 million Redis SET operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 1.15 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the m6gd.medium is its single-core limitation, which restricts its utility for parallelized or multi-threaded applications. However, the inclusion of 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space, enhancing its qualitative cost efficiency for storage-sensitive workloads that do not require massive compute power. This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale database operations, and simple single-threaded tasks or development environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is a general-purpose, ARM-based server powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 4.0 GB of memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. In benchmark testing, the server delivers average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1493.49, though its single-core design limits multi-threaded performance. Memory bandwidth reaches 59.37 GB/sec for cached reads, while database operations achieve 1.18 million Redis SET operations per second. The integrated local NVMe SSD storage offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast local read/write access without high compute demands. The m6gd.medium is suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, single-threaded processing, and small database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.medium is an ARM64 server with 1 vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and 59 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for light general-purpose tasks.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose server featuring a dedicated core and integrated NVMe SSD storage for lightweight, storage-sensitive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Features a dedicated physical core running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and small databases
